title: Zygophlebia cornuta
taxonomy: Zygophlebia cornuta is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It belongs to the genus Zygophlebia, which is a group of epiphytic ferns native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Lindsaeaceae family is a part of the larger class Polypodiopsida, which includes various families of ferns. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Zygophlebia cornuta and other related species. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
